---
title: "Replication — Train"
---

## Goal

Reproduce the four predictive models: Random Forest, XGBoost, MLP
(LSTM fallback), and the Weighted Ensemble. Hyperparameters and the
training pipeline mirror the production `models.py` module.

## Reference pipeline

The canonical training script lives in the production project at
`/Users/labaik/Desktop/Invoices/k/alldocumentsrelatedtoresearch/main.py`.
It chains the modular components:

```text
src/
├── loader.py      load CSVs from data/
├── processor.py   normalise districts, BS→AD calendar, merge
├── engineer.py    lag features, rolling means, log transform
├── models.py      RF + XGB + MLP/LSTM + weighted ensemble
├── evaluator.py   RMSE / MAE / R² / MAPE
├── visualizer.py  the 5 publication figures
└── reporter.py    text-format analysis report
```

To re-run end-to-end:

```bash
cd /Users/labaik/Desktop/Invoices/k/alldocumentsrelatedtoresearch
pip install -r requirements.txt
python main.py
```

This regenerates everything: trained `.pkl` artifacts, the 5 figure
PNGs in `output/figures/`, the metrics CSVs in `output/tables/`, and
the text-format analysis report.

## Trained models

Three trained models are persisted in the production project:

| File | Size | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `rf_model.pkl` | 16 MB | Random Forest (500 trees) |
| `xgb_model.pkl` | 506 KB | XGBoost (300 estimators, depth 6) |
| `mlp_model.pkl` | 289 KB | MLP sequential fallback |
| `scaler_X.pkl` | 1 KB | feature standardisation |
| `scaler_y.pkl` | 0.5 KB | target standardisation |

These are **not committed to the site repo** — they're large and
regenerable from source. Point downstream code at the upstream project
directory or re-train in place using the snippet below.

## Minimal in-site training example

The cell below trains Random Forest and XGBoost on the descriptive
features panel as a smoke test, so this page is independently runnable:

```{python}
#| label: train-smoke
#| code-fold: show

import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

# Load the typhoid CSV and engineer a tiny feature panel inline. This is
# NOT the full pipeline — for that, run main.py in the upstream project.
df = pd.read_csv("../data/typhoid_data_1.csv")
print(f"rows: {len(df):,}, columns: {list(df.columns)[:8]}...")
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Pickled models are not portable across scikit-learn versions.**
The production pickles were trained with the versions pinned in the
upstream project's `requirements.txt`. Re-pickle when upgrading.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
Continue to **[Eval](eval.qmd)** to view the held-out test metrics.
:::
